title: What hides beneath financial hesitation
subtitle: A journal set for tracing the emotion or story underneath a money task you keep deferring.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 45 of the MONEY program. The prompts ask what is actually in the way of a specific deferred action — not why you're busy, but what you're avoiding.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 45 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you can name a specific financial task or conversation you've been avoiding for more than a week.

content.text: A journal set from Day 45 of the MONEY program. The prompts don't diagnose the procrastination — they ask you to look underneath it. The specific task matters less than what's sitting in the way of it.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What's the one financial task on your list that keeps getting deferred?
Name it plainly. Write what you know about why it's there.
When you imagine actually doing that task, what comes up first?
Write the first thing, before you've reasoned it into something respectable.
What emotion or story hides beneath your hesitation to take the next financial step?
Write it plainly — fear of seeing the number, embarrassment, the sense that you should already have done this.
How could you support yourself through that emotion with compassion?
Write what you'd say to a friend in the same spot. Then write it as though it's for you.
What single step, however small, could move you closer to completing this today?
Write it. Write when. The smaller the step, the better.
Adapted from Day 45 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
